test_cases.prompt: I want to think through whether tools shape how we think, and how that connects to atomic note-taking
test_cases.expected_output: Inserts an evergreen placeholder row, retrieves relevant literature/reference notes via FTS5, runs a real discussion where the agent's questions stay grounded to retrieved notes but the user's answers are free, applies the purity rule and sign-off rubric, writes a new evergreen note whose title is none of the individually-cited notes' claims restated.

assertions.query: SELECT count(*) FROM evergreen WHERE status='discussed'

test_cases.prompt: I want to revisit and rewrite an existing evergreen note with new thinking
test_cases.expected_output: Moves the existing row's status back to discussing, re-reads the file from disk, runs discussion again, and on completion replaces the note's content (not just appends), increments iteration.

assertions.query: SELECT iteration FROM evergreen WHERE status='discussed'
